description: The longest ancient beam-style stone bridge in China, renowned as 'No bridge under heaven is as long as this bridge,' an ancient engineering marvel.
An important site for Mazu worship along China's southeastern coast, featuring elegant ancient architecture and bustling incense offerings.
Ruins of ancient city gates from the Song and Yuan dynasties, witnessing the historical development of Quanzhou's ancient city, with high archaeological value.
Remains of the southern main gate of the ancient city during the Song and Yuan dynasties, reflecting the historical changes of Quanzhou.
Stretching coastal line equipped with seaside boardwalks, a high-quality seaside leisure destination near Quanzhou.
The largest comprehensive park in Quanzhou city, featuring picturesque lakes and mountains, ideal for leisurely strolls.
A quintessential example of Minnan red-brick mansion architecture, a masterpiece of overseas Chinese architectural art from the Qing dynasty.
Showcasing the historical and cultural ties between Fujian and Taiwan, offering insights into the development of cross-strait exchanges.
The largest Buddhist temple in Fujian Province, with the famous East and West Twin Pagodas serving as landmarks of Quanzhou's ancient city.
The most historically rich ancient street in Quanzhou, preserving well-maintained Minnan architectural styles and offering a variety of local snacks and delicacies.
